title: Citrusy Crunchy Lazy-Sunday Coriander Seeds Stir-fry
description: This citrusy and crunchy French-inspired dish is perfect for lazy Sundays when you want a quick yet flavorful meal. Made with coriander seeds, this stir-fry combines the warmth of citrus zest with the earthiness of coriander to create a delightful low-sodium-friendly snack or light lunch.
instructions: Slice the lemon in half and squeeze about 3-4 tablespoons of juice into a bowl.
Add white pepper flakes to the bowl, mix well, then set aside.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add cilantro seeds and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Toss in sliced lemon halves and cook until they start to caramelize and release their juice (about 2-3 minutes).
Pour the reserved lemon mixture into the pan, stirring quickly to coat everything evenly.
Drizzle with rice vinegar and stir well for another minute. Remove from heat and serve immediately.
